entry.title: Issue During SAP SuccessFactors AI Role Certification Exam – Stuck on Scoring Screen
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to report a technical issue I experienced during my SAP SuccessFactors AI Role Certification exam.

After completing all questions, I submitted my answers and proceeded to the scoring phase. However, the system became stuck on the scoring/loading screen and did not progress for an extended period of time. Despite waiting, the exam eventually ended with a “time up” status, and I was marked as failed.

I would like to clarify that I had already submitted all my responses and was in the scoring stage when this issue occurred. Therefore, this appears to be a system-related problem rather than an issue with my exam attempt.

I have already submitted a support ticket through SAP Learning, and I also have a backup file of my responses.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue, or is there any additional action I should take to ensure this is properly reviewed?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
When intercepting the F7 key, try to check if a certain control is currently on the screen. For example, look for the control ID of the warehouse number input field, and only continue with suppressing the F7 action if it's present.

If this is not acceptable (because the same control ID is also on other screens), then try finding something else that is unique to the initial screen.

entry.title: Production order PR issue using sub contract
entry.content:
Hi Team,
I am currently encountering an issue with PR creation for the subcontracting process in Production Operations when using an external processing control key, and I am unable to proceed beyond this step.

I used the YBP2 Control Key and created a PIR for material T610‑0518‑A, ensuring that both the Material Group (L004) in the PIR and the Material Master match. The material is also assigned to L004 in the master data, and the procurement setup includes a production operation.

However, despite this alignment, I am still receiving a Material Group mismatch error.

For reference, in MRP 2 I am using Planning Strategy 20 (Make‑to‑Order) and the Procurement Type “X” (both internal and external procurement).

and used 30 sub contract in MRP 2.

entry.title: How does delta works for Datasphere Transformation Flow?
entry.content:
Hello,

At the moment, I do not have sufficient time to conduct a proof of concept (POC) in our DSP system, as I am currently engaged with other priorities. However, in preparation for future DSP-related tasks, I would like to understand how delta handling works in a Transformation Flow.

Scenario:
We have three Local Tables populated via Replication Flows:

  • LT1 – EKKO
  • LT2 – EKPO
  • LT3 – EKBE

These Local Tables are joined in a Graphical View based on defined business logic. The resulting View is then persisted (staged) into a new Local Table.

Question:
How does the delta mechanism behave for a Transformation Flow that extracts data from a View and loads it into a Local Table?

Note: I have not yet conducted a POC or performed detailed research on this topic.

entry.title: Re: Road map for SAP .Net connector for .Net core 10
entry.content:
Hi, 

We are aware of the fact that the next LTS version .NET 10 is available. Before this runtime can be supported a validation needs to happen that NCo works as expected with this runtime. This has not happened yet. The support strategy for NCo is described in SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/2786882, and whenever it will be changed (adding new OS versions, .NET runtimes, dropping them, it will be updated accordingly). A concrete commitment is not possible in this forum. However, I can recommend to add the mentioned note as favorite note so that you get a notification once anything is changing with regard to the support strategy. 

Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: Re: Limit warehouse order for PI document
entry.content:
I understand this is an older thread, but I am providing this information in case others arrive here with the same query.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to use RDOCID as the field to limit warehouse order size, as previously suggested by Jurgen. This is because the actual physical inventory document is mapped elsewhere, meaning the RDOCID remains empty during the warehouse order limit calculation.

However, you can use the indicator "Do Not Split Warehouse Orders Between PI Documents" in SPRO -> EWM -> Master Data -> Define Warehouse Number Control:


*It might just be me, but I think the indicator's description is a bit misleading since we want to split the warehouse orders between physical inventory documents.

In some releases, the indicator could be missing from config so you would need to implement SAP note 3517096 to enable it.

Cheers,
Jon

entry.title: Teamwork Makes the AI Dream Work: Overview of Key Data Practitioner Roles
entry.content:
The transition to AI-driven business processes presents significant potential, but quality is directly influenced by the underlying data foundation. For smaller companies, the boundaries of a data foundation may be confined to a single database management system. Conversely, for larger enterprises, relevant data is more widely dispersed. Regardless of the size and scope, the expectations placed on the data team for this interconnected web of business context remain constant. Transactional data, master data, IoT data, unstructured data, multi-model data, and all other data types must be integrated into the IT landscape and optimized in a manner that facilitates multi-use and multi-purpose consumption.

A crucial factor in achieving success is the data platform's capacity to support the specific needs of each data professional while fulfilling the insatiable demands of artificial intelligence, analytics, and custom applications. As part of the launch of the data professional’s community, this wiki document along with the related blog posts offer resources that enable you, the data practitioner, to utilize SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). As the #datapro community develops, resources will be available to help you optimally use the platform to address the data requirements of the business, IT systems, and the rest of your data ecosystem.

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Overview

SAP Business Data Cloud is a SaaS cloud solution that connects data from SAP applications and external sources while preserving the business semantics necessary for trustworthy analytics and artificial intelligence. SAP BDC provides an intuitive foundation for data engineering, modeling, and governed sharing across regions and platforms. The solution supports both virtualization and replication, offers catalog and lineage for transparency, insightful analytics, and delivers the performance required for mixed workloads spanning structured and semi-structured data.

The portfolio includes core services and complementary capabilities such zero copy data sharing. The individual solutions include:

  • SAP Datasphere delivers a strategic business data fabric with semantic modeling, data virtualization and movement, cataloging, and controlled sharing.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud covers business intelligence, planning, and augmented analytics on curated models.
  • SAP Databricks integrates the Databricks Lakehouse with SAP semantics to streamline feature engineering, notebooks-driven exploration, and production machine learning pipelines.
  • SAP Business Warehouse supplies enterprise warehousing and transformation content that aligns with the cloud data fabric.
  • Intelligent Applications augment business processes using the data fabric and AI to adapt and learn from your data. With your business context, these applications can act on your behalf to transform business outcomes.
  • SAP Snowflake expands the data landscape by integrating its elastic platform with SAP business context for simultaneous analysis of SAP and non-SAP data at scale.
  • SAP Master Data Governance provides ready-to-run, domain-specific, master data management, so you can locally own and consolidate or centrally create, change, and distribute trusted master data across your enterprise system landscape.
  • SAP HANA Cloud provides a high-performance database and lake, supporting relational, document, graph, spatial, and time-series workloads with elastic scale. Note: SAP HANA Cloud is a BTP service but very relevant to data professionals and this community.

SAP BDC Blog Posts for Data Professionals 

For every team member to perform their best, access to the most relevant resources is a must. Consider the following set of curated content that could be relevant to your role on the data team.

Business Analyst

Business Analysts sit at the intersection of data and decision-making, translating complex information into insights that guide day-to-day operations and strategic initiatives. Their work depends on speed, consistency, and clarity, yet they are often slowed down by manual preparation, unclear definitions, or heavy reliance on technical teams.

 SAP Business Data Cloud equips Business Analysts with a unified suite of curated data products, intelligent applications, and planning and analytics tools, so they can move faster and act with confidence. Harmonized, contextual data stays consistent across domains while out-the-box dashboards and AI-driven insights are tailored to each line of business. Scenario modeling helps connect insights to decisions, and AI copilots like SAP Joule automate routine tasks so analysts can focus on higher-value work. The result: more agile, scalable analytics where insights don’t just inform decisions but actively drive them.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Business Analyst to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Steward

A Data Steward is a business-savvy, analytical professional with deep expertise in data, particularly within a specific domain or line of business. Their role is to ensure the availability of well-governed data that is easily discoverable, reliable, up-to-date, and aligned with corporate and compliance standards.

SAP Business Data Cloud enhances this critical function by providing key features such as metadata management, including a centralized enterprise data catalog that tracks attributes, quality, lineage, and sensitivity. The catalog also enables governance and controlled access to data products. Additionally, for master data stewards, SAP Master Data Governance offers advanced capabilities for data quality management, consolidation, and centralized governance of business-critical master data domains.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Steward to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team, along with the lines of business they support. 

Data Engineer

Data engineers facilitate the transfer of data from its source to its intended destinations. These data pipelines can become intricate, encompassing a substantial volume of data, a multitude of destinations, and complex logic. Upon the completion of data movement, this role actively participates in the development of the enterprise data model.

SAP Business Data Cloud streamlines data movement by serving as multiple roles, including a “control center” for data transfers. The act of centralization, coupled with data tiering, simplifies redundancy and complexity – resulting in the delivery of data in a timeframe necessary for a modern business process. Regardless of whether the data gravity is directed towards SAP Business Warehouse, SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Databricks, SAP Snowflake, or any combination, data engineers assemble the business data fabric that fuels the company's applications, analytics, and artificial intelligence solutions.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Engineer to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Modeler

Data modelers design and govern the business-semantic structures that make enterprise data consistent, reusable, and analytics ready. Day to day, they define entities, relationships, and keys; harmonize definitions across sources; document lineage and metadata; enforce data quality and security constraints; and evolve conceptual, logical, and physical models to support changing business needs.

SAP Business Data Cloud streamlines this work by providing a governed, business-semantic modeling layer in SAP Datasphere to define domains, entities, and relationships once and publish them as trusted data products. It connects to SAP and non-SAP sources with federation, centralizes glossary, lineage, and policy controls for impact analysis and access management, and integrates with platforms like Databricks and Snowflake so transformations can be pushed down and scaled without sacrificing governance.

Visit this blog post (coming soon) for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Modeler to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team. 

Data Scientist

Data scientists utilize historical patterns to anticipate future trends, enabling informed business decisions in the present. This role requires access to high-quality data to construct and train models employing statistical analysis and intricate logic.

SAP Business Data Cloud facilitates the acquisition of high-quality data from both SAP and non-SAP sources through SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Databricks, and SAP Snowflake, thereby establishing a comprehensive business data fabric. Additionally, this role leverages the advanced SQL, notebook, and data science capabilities of these systems to disseminate complex models utilizing all pertinent enterprise data.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Data Scientist to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team.

Developer

Developers bridge the technical gap between business process and backend systems. This role can include any combination of specialty such as mobile applications, web-sites, desktop applications, Api’s, chatbots, or even agentic AI solutions. Incumbents in this role must equally understand logic, design, and data.

SAP Business Data Cloud provide the critical data a developer needs to deploy the best solution to the business. SAP HANA Cloud is a best of bread database for industrial class, high-performance data platforms designed to run business processes in real-time. This role specifically takes advantage of this in-memory, multi-model platform by utilizing every type of data within the organization to provide an experience that is engaging and intuitive. Solutions built using SAP HANA Cloud are also natively part of a largely business data fabric – bidirectionally sharing data so that all aspects of the business can operate in real-time and in unison.

Visit this blog post for more insight on how BDC enables the Developer to contribute to the success of the data and analytics team.

Conclusion

Teamwork makes the AI dream work when everyone shares standards, clear governance, and a common data foundation. With SAP BDC, remove the friction across the data lifecycle for impactful business outcomes.

Kindly join and then bookmark the Data Professionals community for relevant resources, events, and discussions.

entry.title: Limit Item PO: Changes to Expected value: Change indicator not displayed in the Standard PO Print
entry.content:
We are using the ‘Limit’ Item functionality in the purchase order and need to see the changes to the ‘Expected Value’ shown as a change in the Standard PO Print.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Limit Item PO: Changes to Expected value: Change indi...
entry.content:
In order to add the Expected Value in the Purchase Order Output you need to add a new text in the Define Change Texts SSCUI 102271 with a custom ID, then you need to add the EXPECTEDOVERALLLIMITAMOUNT in the Fields Relevant to Printouts of Changes configuration SSCUI 102272 and map to the newly created ID. This will triggered the change with the correct text. See more information on the blog post: How to generate purchase order output on change of a purchase order?.

Thanks,
Diéssica

entry.title: How to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse tasks?
entry.content:
Hi,

The business wants to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse tasks. Could you please help with the required setup for this? I’m unable to find any option to configure this in the Output Parameter Determination.

Thanks.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger the putaway list based on warehouse ta...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The putaway list includes the warehouse order and warehouse tasks needed for the putaway process. It also contains the warehouse order and inbound delivery order as the bar code on header level.

The putaway list is relevant for open warehouse orders with warehouse process type S110 (Putaway) or S115 (Putaway from Production), and it's issued when creating tasks in the Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries app and the Change Inbound Deliveries - Production app.

The supported channel is printout.

To adapt the layout to your needs, copy the form template EWM_PUTAWAY_LIST_2.

For further details, please check the document below: 

Putaway List | SAP Help Portal.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
Re. Another question for what reason it is better to use JSM in case of Heavy iFlow logic?
How can the type of the queue have an impact on the processing of the iFlow?

Ans.
First reason - decoupling.
You consume from Event Mesh and immediately write to internal JMS queue, then finish the iFlow as COMPLETED. Event Mesh message is acknowledged quickly, so you’re not holding broker resources while doing long processing.

Second reason - better retry control.
Heavy flows tend to hit transient issues. Then exponential backoff matters that is covered only in JMS, not AMQP adapter.

entry.title: Price Amount Recalculation in Peppol E-Invoicing for SAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In the world of electronic invoicing, especially within the Peppol network, precision is paramount. A single misplaced decimal can lead to rejected invoices, delayed payments, and frustrated business partners. One of the frequently discussed topics is the calculation of the PriceAmount field in UBL invoices. This blog post aims to shed light on why SAP's standard solution for Document and Reporting Compliance (e-invoicing) behaves the way it does, and how you can tailor it to your specific business needs.

Scope Note: This blog post covers the SAP S/4HANA on-premise implementation and S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using BAdI EDOC_ADAPTOR, and assuming the latest version as per 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview - SAP for Me is implemented. 
This blog post refers only to eDocuments which were created out of Sales & Distribution Billing documents, as those allow price conditions as allowances or surcharges.
For SAP Public Edition, ABAP Environment, the equivalent BAdI is EDOC_ADAPTOR_CLOUD. The overall approach is similar, but backend APIs to format the amounts in the XML may differ and are not covered here.

As this behavior is cross-country enabled and as the mapping for CrossIndustryInvoice for Germany is implemented on top of the Peppol mapping, this is also valid for Germany / ZUGFeRD process as outlined here.

What is the Issue? The Peppol Rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120

At the heart of the matter lies a critical Peppol validation rule: PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. This rule mandates that the Invoice line net amount must be consistent with the quantity and price. In simple terms, the following equation must hold true (within a defined rounding tolerance):

LineExtensionAmount = InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)

If an invoice line includes discounts or surcharges (AllowanceCharge), the formula expands:

LineExtensionAmount = (InvoicedQuantity * (PriceAmount / BaseQuantity)) - Allowances + Charges

If this mathematical check fails, the Peppol Access Point will reject the invoice. The ultimate goal of the SAP standard solution is to ensure every generated invoice is compliant and successfully transmitted through the network.

Why SAP Cannot Deliver the Perfect Calculation in Every Case

The challenge arises because different businesses represent prices and discounts in different ways. SAP's e-invoicing solution must balance flexibility with the strict need for compliance.

SAP's standard mapping tries to generate a valid invoice that will be accepted by the Peppol Network. It does this by recalculating the PriceAmount if it detects a potential violation of the R120 rule. This often happens in two common scenarios:

  1. Hidden Discounts/Surcharges: Many businesses prefer not to show item-level discounts or surcharges separately in the final XML, at least not all of them. At the same time, they want the PriceAmount to reflect the net price. If the discount/surcharge conditions are not mapped to the <cac:AllowanceCharge> element in the XML, the standard PriceAmount (often derived from a gross price condition) will not match the final LineExtensionAmount. SAP's logic detects this and recalculates the PriceAmount to absorb these "hidden" values.

  2. High-Precision Prices: For low-priced items sold in bulk, kWh sold in Utilities, or items with prices derived from complex calculations, the calculated unit price can have many decimal places (e.g., €7.39933 per piece). The UBL standard allows for this, but historically, currency fields were often handled with only two decimal places. SAP's standard logic scales the price, but keeps two digits, avoiding rounding errors that would violate rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120. So instead of showing 7.39933 / 1 piece, it might show 7399.33 / 1000 pieces. While mathematically identical, this can be confusing for human readers of the invoice.

This scaling logic is what customers may find "weird". However, it's a safe-by-default mechanism to guarantee compliance.

Tailoring the Calculation with a BAdI Implementation

The good news is that you have full control. Using the Business Add-In (BAdI) EDOC_ADAPTOR, you can implement your own logic to override the standard scaling behavior.

The best place to do this is in the BAdI method IF_EDOC_ADAPTOR~SET_OUTPUT_DATA. This method is called after SAP has performed its standard mapping, giving you the final data structure just before it's converted to XML.

Here is a sample implementation that rescales the price based on a simple rule: if the invoiced quantity is less than 1000, use a BaseQuantity of 1; otherwise, use 1000. This provides a more predictable and readable output, and can be fine-tuned to your needs.

CLASS zcl_edoc_adaptor_scaling IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_edoc_adaptor~set_output_data.
    " This BAdI implementation rescales the PriceAmount and BaseQuantity
    " according to a custom logic, overriding the standard SAP scaling.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS:
      <fs_invoice_data>   TYPE any,
      <lt_invoice_line>   TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
      <fs_line>           TYPE any,
      <fs_price>          TYPE any,
      <fs_price_amount>   TYPE any,
      <fs_base_quantity>  TYPE any,
      <fs_inv_quantity>   TYPE any.

    DATA:
      lv_current_price     TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_current_base_qty  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_invoiced_quantity TYPE fklmg,
      lv_unit_price        TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_base_quantity TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10,
      lv_new_price_amount  TYPE p LENGTH 16 DECIMALS 10.

    IF iv_interface_id CS 'UBL_INVOICE_'.
      ASSIGN cs_output_data TO <fs_invoice_data>.
      IF <fs_invoice_data> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICE-INVOICE_LINE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_invoice_data> TO <lt_invoice_line>.
      IF <lt_invoice_line> IS NOT ASSIGNED. RETURN. ENDIF.

      LOOP AT <lt_invoice_line> ASSIGNING <fs_line>.
        " Get current Price, BaseQuantity, and InvoicedQuantity
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_price>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'INVOICED_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_line> TO <fs_inv_quantity>.
        IF <fs_price> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_inv_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'PRICE_AMOUNT-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_price_amount>.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT 'BASE_QUANTITY-BASE-BASE-CONTENT' OF STRUCTURE <fs_price> TO <fs_base_quantity>.

        IF <fs_price_amount> IS NOT ASSIGNED OR <fs_base_quantity> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.
        
        lv_current_price = <fs_price_amount>.
        lv_current_base_qty = <fs_base_quantity>.
        lv_invoiced_quantity = <fs_inv_quantity>.

        IF lv_current_base_qty = 0.
          CONTINUE.
        ENDIF.

        " 1. Calculate the true unit price from the existing values
        lv_unit_price = lv_current_price / lv_current_base_qty.

        " 2. *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC STARTS HERE ***
        IF lv_invoiced_quantity < 1000.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1.
        ELSE.
          lv_new_base_quantity = 1000.
        ENDIF.
        " *** CUSTOMER-SPECIFIC SCALING LOGIC ENDS HERE ***

        " 3. Calculate the new PriceAmount based on the new BaseQuantity
        lv_new_price_amount = lv_unit_price * lv_new_base_quantity.

        " 4. Write the new values back and format for XML output
        <fs_price_amount>  = lv_new_price_amount.
        <fs_base_quantity> = lv_new_base_quantity.

        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_price_amount> ).
        cl_edoc_map_aif=>format_negative_amount( CHANGING cv_amount = <fs_base_quantity> ).

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Summary

Navigating Peppol compliance requires a careful balance between standard processes and business-specific needs. While SAP's default behavior for PriceAmount recalculation prioritizes compliance with rule PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 to ensure your invoices are accepted, it may not always align with your desired representation.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance is King: The primary reason for the recalculation is to comply with PEPPOL-EN16931-R120 and prevent invoice rejection.
  • You Have Choices: You can configure how discounts and surcharges appear. Either show them separately in <cac:AllowanceCharge> elements or absorb them into a net PriceAmount. The latter often triggers the recalculation.
  • You Have Control: The EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI provides a powerful, upgrade-safe way to implement your own pricing representation logic, overriding the default scaling to produce invoices that are both compliant and easy for your customers to understand.

entry.title: New Learning Journey + Knowledge Badge: Joule & Business AI in SAP CX
entry.content:
On behalf of the Product learning team, I am excited to announce the launch of a new learning journey with a knowledge badge : 
Exploring Joule and Business AI in SAP Customer Experience.

This journey helps SAP CX professionals understand how Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, and SAP Business AI enhance productivity and transform customer experience processes across sales, service, commerce, and marketing. Learners will explore how AI-driven insights streamline decision-making, improve implementation efficiency, and support the evolving role of the next-generation CX consultant.  It ends in a self paced assessment, that helps obtain a knowledge badge.

🌟What This Learning Journey Covers

This learning journey provides a comprehensive introduction to:

  • The role of Joule and Business AI across SAP Customer Experience solutions  
  • How AI capabilities enhance CX business processes end‑to‑end  
  • Key Joule agent use cases supporting sales, service, commerce, and marketing scenarios  
  • How consultants can leverage Joule for faster solution design, configuration, troubleshooting, and advisory work  
  • How embedded AI accelerates implementations and supports the skill set of the Next Generation CX Consultant  

These insights equip learners to explore AI-enabled workflows and elevate their mastery of the SAP CX portfolio.

🎯Who Should Join?

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • SAP CX consultants
  • Solution architects
  • Business users and CX practitioners
  • Anyone preparing to work with AI-driven customer experience capabilities

🧠 How This Learning Journey Helps You

After completing the modules, learners will be able to:

  • Explain how Joule and Business AI enhance customer experience processes
  • Identify how Joule agents assist in real business scenarios
  • Understand how consultants can use AI to accelerate design, configuration, and optimization tasks
  • Navigate AI-driven productivity tools embedded across the SAP CX suite
     

🎓Earn a Knowledge Badge

Upon completing the learning journey, learners become eligible to attempt a Knowledge Badge assessment.

Here’s how it works:

  • You will answer 30 questions in a mix of multiple-choice and multiple-response formats.
  • The exam evaluates your understanding of Joule, Business AI concepts, and the scenarios covered in the learning journey.
  • Successful completion awards you an official SAP Knowledge Badge, which can be showcased on your SAP profile and shared externally.
  • You can attempt the questions any number of times.

This is a great way to validate your understanding and demonstrate your readiness for AI‑powered CX roles.

📚Start Learning Today

The learning journey is completely free and available now on SAP Learning.
Access it directly here:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-joule-and-business-ai-in-sap-customer-experience

entry.title: Business Processes Management Learning Series 2026 (Open to students and professors!)
entry.content:
Business Process Management – Guided Learning from the SAP Learning Hub

This course offers a structured introduction to Business Process Management based on carefully selected chapters from the SAP Learning Hub’s Learning Journeys. The hosts have cheery-picked the most relevant and impactful chapter for you. The selected content focuses on understanding, navigating, and analyzing business processes in an SAP context, combining theoretical foundations with system-oriented perspectives. All chapters are completed by students in a self-paced learning mode, allowing for a high degree of flexibility and individual learning speed.

To complement the self-paced format, the course includes weekly online sessions. Each weekly live session begins with 25 minutes of new content, including a short poll, followed by a 5-minute breakout exercise to apply key concepts. Afterwards, another 15 minutes of new content are delivered, again supported by a quick poll. The session concludes with a 10-minute Q&A covering current and previous topics, as well as brief preparation guidance for the following week, ensuring continuity and structured learning progress.

A Flexible Learning Series– Guided and Hands-On

The program is designed to be manageable alongside your studies or other commitments. In total, it takes about 40 hours. Around 30 hours are dedicated to the self-paced Learning material from the SAP Learning Hub, which you can complete whenever it suits you. The pace is up to you, and you can revisit the content whenever you like.

In addition, there are 11 live Zoom sessions, each lasting one hour (Thursdays, 4:00 pm - 5:00 pm CET) These take place weekly over the course of the program and give you the chance to hear explanations from an BPM and ERP experts, ask questions, and learn from real-world stories. This steady rhythm ensures that learning stays consistent while never becoming overwhelming.

To make the learning even more interactive, you will also get access to live practice system like  SAP S/4HANA , SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX where you can complete guided exercises and try out processes for yourself. This way, the course connects theory and application in a very concrete way.

Boost Your Skills with Practice, Networking, and Certification

In this learning series, you will gain a solid understanding of Business Process Manangement. You will also meet other participants with similar interests and exchange ideas during the weekly sessions. The interaction with an BPM and ERP experts and your peers makes the learning experience lively and collaborative.

Please note: This program is exclusively available to lecturers and students from officially accredited educational institutions. Registrants need to prove their eligibility by registering with an e-mail address referring to an academic e-mail account!

Overall, the course offers a mixture of knowledge, practice, and community. It is an exciting chance to dive into the world of SAP, learn how companies manage their processes, and develop skills that will stay valuable in the future.

entry.title: Re: Waitlist Notification
entry.content:
Hi @EricGreen ,

Is the setting 'Auto Enroll from Waitlist' enabled at the class's record > Details tab?

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0003481348
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/managing-sap-successfactors-learning-for-administrators/enabling-waitlists?version=2511
    • Set Auto Enroll from Waitlist to Yes if you want to automatically fill people from the waitlist when a seat opens up in the course.
    • Set Auto Enroll from Waitlist to No if you want to manually control how seats are filled from the waitlist when people withdraw from courses.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Transport changes not available in QA but successful...
entry.content:
you can also test the connections in stms (system overview > sap system > check > connectiontest) 

if there the check is ok, there is no authorization problem with the user tmsadm. authorization S_A.TMSADM for user tmsadm is sufficient.

possibly you see in su53 an error because of missing authorization for "RFC_PING" but this is not causing your described behaviour. RFC_PING missing is not a problem for stms environment, just an authorization check in sm59 connections for stms would be failing with authorization error. 

furthermore you can check especially the import logs of the transport and look out for your missing object in the particular logs. this should also give you a hint.

br

entry.title: Re: Financial Statement Version for Profit & Loss Format...
entry.content:
Pls refer this https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/financial-statement-version/qaq-p/2961226

These two nodes, Net Result: Profit and Net Result: Loss, should be included under the Balance Sheet section. This ensures that the period’s profit or loss is carried over correctly, allowing the Balance Sheet to balance.

entry.title: Experience Updates in SAP SuccessFactors: Improving Navigation and Consistency
entry.content:
SAP continues to evolve the user experience across its cloud solutions to provide a more consistent and intuitive way for users to navigate applications. As part of this effort, SAP SuccessFactors introduces updates to several foundational interface components aligned with the SAP design system. 

These updates focus on areas that users interact with frequently, including the Shell Bar, Side Navigation, User Menu, and User Settings. The goal is to simplify navigation, improve orientation across applications, and provide a consistent structure for common user actions. Users benefit from clearer navigation patterns, easier access to common actions, and a more structured way to manage personal preferences. 

Shell Bar 

The Shell Bar is the topmost area of the screen and provides access to global actions such as search, notifications, help, and user profile. Because it appears across all parts of the application, it plays an important role in helping users orient themselves and access common functionality. 

The updated shell bar introduces a standardized structure aligned with the SAP design system. 

  • A product name field has been introduced to clearly identify the application users are currently working in. This helps users quickly understand the product context when navigating between different SAP solutions. 
  • Global actions such as Search, Joule, Notifications, Help, and the User Profile now follow a consistent placement, improving predictability when moving between SAP applications. 
  • Some elements have also been reorganized. The context switcher has moved from the shell bar to the User Menu, consolidating user-related actions in one place. These updates create a cleaner and more predictable top navigation experience. 

Side Navigation 

Primary navigation is now consistently placed on the left side of the screen, following a pattern identical across many SAP products. This approach makes efficient use of modern screen sizes and provides a structured way to access different areas of the application. 

  • The side navigation reflects the structure of the product, helping users understand where they are and how to move between different sections. 
  • The navigation supports several display modes: 
  • Expanded mode, where the navigation menu is fully visible 
  • Collapsed mode, which provides more space for application content 
  • Navigation items care organized hierarchically and grouped logically, allowing complex product areas to present their structure in a clear and accessible way. 

By standardizing the side navigation across applications, users benefit from a more familiar and consistent navigation model. 

User Menu 

The User Menu, accessed from the avatar in the shell bar, has been redesigned to provide a clearer structure for user-specific actions. 

  • The updated design offers more space to display the user’s photo and identifying information, making it easier to recognize the current user context. 
  • The structure of the menu has also been standardized. The menu now consistently includes: 
    • User information 
    • Settings 
    • Product-specific user actions 
    • Legal information 
    • About 
    • Sign Out 
  • The Sign Out option is now placed in a clearly separated section, making it easier to locate when needed. 
  • For users working with multiple employments or contexts, the context switcher is now available in the User Menu, consolidating user-related actions in a single location. 

User Settings 

User settings may include preferences such as language, accessibility, or notification behavior.  With the latest updates, settings no longer open as a separate full page. Instead, they now appear in a dialog, allowing users to review or adjust preferences without leaving their current workflow. 

  • The settings interface has also been reorganized into a standardized structure. This consistent structure helps users locate and manage preferences more easily across applications. 
  • To keep the main settings interface simple, more advanced configuration options, such as proxy settings or delegation, can be accessed through a More Settings option.

entry.title: Fiori Elements Navigation Patterns Showcase
entry.content:
Introduction

There are multiple ways to navigate from a Fiori Elements application to another application (outbound navigation).

While the SAPUI5 documentation explains each annotation in detail, it is often focused on code snippets, making it difficult to visualize how each navigation pattern actually behaves in the UI.

To address this, I created a sample repository that demonstrates various Fiori Elements navigation patterns along with their actual UI behavior.

https://github.com/miyasuta/fiori-navigation-patterns

This repository focuses on navigation patterns that can be implemented using annotations only.
Controller extension-based approaches are out of scope and may be covered separately in the future.

In this blog, I will provide an overview of this sample project.

 

Project Structure

This project has a simple structure:

  • Backend: CAP Node.js
  • Frontend: Two Fiori Elements applications
    • nav-source: Navigation source app
    • nav-target: Navigation target app

The nav-source app demonstrates various navigation patterns, while the nav-target app shows how navigation context is received.

The README includes:

  • UI screenshots for each navigation pattern
  • Annotation configurations
  • Verification steps

Figure 1: Excerpt from the project README illustrating navigation patterns and documentation

You can either read the README or run the project locally to explore the behavior yourself.

git clone https://github.com/miyasuta/fiori-navigation-patterns.git
cd fiori-navigation-patterns
npm install
cds watch

After starting the app, open the FLP sandbox (/launchpad) and launch the application.

 

Organizing Navigation Patterns

In this repository, navigation is organized from two perspectives:

  • Group A — Navigation Trigger: How navigation is initiated
  • Group B — Navigation Context: How to control what data is passed to the target application

Separating these two aspects makes it easier to understand how Fiori Elements navigation works.

 

Group A — Navigation Triggers

Group A introduces common patterns for triggering navigation:

  • Semantic Link
  • Intent-Based Navigation (IBN) Button
  • Direct IBN Link
  • URL Link
  • Row-click navigation replacement

All patterns can be tested directly in the nav-source List Report.

Figure 2: Navigation trigger patterns displayed in the nav-source List Report

In addition to the main patterns, the repository also covers a few supplementary behaviors, such as:

  • Dynamic semantic objects (supported only for semantic links)
  • Conditional visibility of navigation actions
  • Hiding specific actions from semantic object popovers

 

Group B — Navigation Context

Group B explains how to modify parameter mappings and control which data is passed (or not passed) to the target application.

In particular, it covers how to:

  • Map local fields to different parameter names
  • Pass association properties
  • Exclude sensitive or irrelevant fields from the navigation context

These patterns help you control exactly what information is transferred during navigation and how it is interpreted by the target application.

 

Which Navigation Pattern Should You Use?

Since there are multiple options, it is not always obvious which pattern to choose.

This repository provides a simple decision guideline:

Figure 3: Decision guideline for choosing the appropriate navigation pattern

The diagram above provides a practical guideline for selecting the appropriate navigation pattern based on your scenario.

 

Conclusion

Fiori Elements navigation is powerful but can be difficult to understand due to the number of available patterns.

This sample project organizes navigation into:

  • Navigation triggers
  • Navigation context

and allows you to explore both aspects through working examples.

I hope this repository helps you better understand and design navigation in your Fiori Elements applications.

entry.title: DRC Restriction – Support Document (CS) with Inventory Accounts in Public Edition 2602 (Colombia)
entry.content:
Hello,

The current behavior for Support Document Colombia Localization is as follows:

  • The CS document is processed correctly when postings are made against expense accounts.
  • However, a limitation occurs when a supplier not required to issue invoices is involved in transactions related to inventory (raw materials).

Specific scenario:

In cases where there are price differences between the purchase order and the final value of the support document, adjustments are required that impact inventory accounts.

Under this scenario, when sending the document to DIAN via Document Compliance & Reporting (DRC), an error is triggered indicating that document type CS does not allow postings to material/inventory accounts.

Requirement:

We need to enable the use of standard document types KR (Vendor Invoice) or KG (Vendor Credit Memo) for Support Documents, and ensure they are accepted by DRC for submission to DIAN.

Currently, when using KR or KG, the document is posted correctly in the ERP; however, the submission to DIAN fails due to a validation error in DRC.

We would appreciate your guidance on whether there are any SAP-recommended alternatives to handle this type of scenario (inventory adjustments within support documents).

Error message using documents types  KG or KG.

entry.title: Joule Skill for S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I would like to take the example of Procurement Risk analyzer agent for this discussion: https://api.sap.com/package/eu10.storecontentdev.procurementriskanalyzeragent/overview

It uses API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (SAP_COM_0008) and API_PURCHASEORDER_2 (SAP_COM_0053)

S/4HANA Public Cloud only allows any other system to interact with it, using these Communication Arrangements. Communication arrangements uses Basic or Certificate based authentications, using Technical Users.

I would like to know, how does a Joule Skill connect to an API deployed on S/4HANA Public Cloud to fetch or create data?

  • If using Communication arrangement using a Technical user, then, how does the data access control work? For eg. How to restrict the purchase org. information for which the user is not authorized for?
  • If using Principal Propagation, then how to setup Communication Arrangements to allow access to users using Principal Propagation?

entry.title: Re: New fixes and features in the Pipeline for Cloud Integration package with the Dec 2025 shipment
entry.content:
@ensync thanks for pointing me to the issue, I also responded to an incident ticket already, I identified the root cause and have republished the inbound package with the fix, the fix is just a one liner where I initiate the property Pid, this unfortunately slipped through my tests because in my tests I changed the externalized parameter CustomXPid_ResolveVirtualReceiver from the default false to true because I also tested use cases with so called virtual receiver, in this case Pid is preset and the error won't occur. Unfortunately, I missed to test with the default setting false, as said I fixed the error, retested and it now looks fine. You need to wait for the next version published, the fix will come with inbound package version 1.0.7 and the generic idoc inbound flow will have version 1.0.8. If you can't wait for the fix, you can configure the parameter to true, then the error won't occur but it will carry out an additional check to determine the partner id which may be in vain, at least for the time being it will fix the error.

My colleague and I will soon publish blog posts describing the new features which came with the new version, actually a more flexible landscape stage capability and support for inbound IDoc with partner type not equal LS and support for so called virtual receiver, a feature which is actually supported in SAP Process Orchestration and hence implemented here as well. Also, standard documentation will be updated soon. So, stay tuned.

Sorry for all the inconvenience

Alex

entry.title: Document Grounding Setup in Joule: Building Smarter Joule Agents with AI Core and S3
entry.content:
Overview:

This guide provides comprehensive instructions for setting up and configuring document grounding capabilities in Joule Studio. Document grounding enables your Joule agent to search through documents, retrieve relevant information, and provide contextually accurate responses based on your organization's specific content.

By following this guide, you will:

  • Set up the required SAP BTP infrastructure (Object Store and SAP AI Core)
  • Create and configure a document grounding resource group
  • Build a Joule agent with document grounding capabilities
  • Apply best practices for prompting agents that use document grounding

Prerequisite:

Before you begin, ensure you have the following:

Requirement

Description

URL's

SAP BTP Global Account

Active global account with administrator access

SAP BTP Global Account

SAP BTP Subaccount

Dedicated subaccount for Joule Studio and AI services

SAP BTP Subaccount

AI Core Entitlement

'aicore` service entitlement assigned to your subaccount

SAP AI Core

Object Store Service

S3-compatible object store service access

Object Store on AWS

SAP Build Process Automation

Joule Studio Capability and Grounding services

SAP Build Process Automation 

Technical Knowledge:

  • Basic understanding of SAP BTP destinations
  • Familiarity with REST API concepts
  • Understanding of OAuth2 authentication
  • Basic knowledge of document embedding and vector search concepts

Architecture Overview:

Components:

  • Joule Agent: Your AI agent that interacts with users and utilizes document grounding
  • Document Grounding Tool: Enables semantic search across embedded document chunks
  • SAP AI Core: Provides embedding models and vector database capabilities for document grounding
  • Object Store (S3): Stores your documents and their metadata
  • SAP BTP Build Process Automation: is a comprehensive low-code/no-code platform enables Joule studio
  • SAP BTP: The underlying platform hosting all services

Setup Document Grounding tool in Joule Studio:

Step 1: Configure SAP BTP Destinations

1.1 Configure AI Core Destination:

a. In your subaccount, navigate to Connectivity → Destinations
b. Click New Destination
c. Configure with the following settings:

 

Field

Value

Notes

Name

ai-core

Use a descriptive name

Type

HTTP

-

URL

`<serviceurls>` 

From service key

Proxy Type

Internet

-

Authentication

'OAuth2ClientCredentials'-

Client ID

'<clientid>'

From service key

Client Secret

'<clientsecret>'From service key

Token Service URL

`<url>/oauth/token`

From service key
Token Service URL Type'Dedicated'-

Add the following additional property to the destination in order to appear it in Joule Studio:

 `sap-joule-studio-document-grounding-ai-core` `true`

!!! tip "Verify Destination"
 After creating the destination, use the Check Connection button to verify it's configured correctly.

1.2 Create Object Store (S3) Destination:

a. In your subaccount, navigate to Connectivity → Destinations
b. Click New Destination
c. Configure with the following settings:

 

Field

Value

Notes

Name

'object-store-s3'

Use a descriptive name

Type

HTTP

-

URL

'https://<host>'

Your S3 host URL

Proxy Type

Internet

-

Authentication

'OAuth2ClientCredentials'-

Client ID

'<access_key_id>'

Your S3 access key

Client Secret

'<secret_access_key>'Your S3 secret 

Token Service URL

'https://<host>'

Same as URL

Token Service URL Type'Dedicated'-

Add the following additional property to the destination in order to appear it in Joule Studio:

Key

Value

Description

sap-joule-studio-document-grounding-object-store

true

Identifies destination for document grounding

region

'<your-region>'

S3 region (e.g., `us-east-1`)

bucket

`<your-bucket>`

S3 bucket name

username

`<username>`

Username if required 

type'S3'

Specifies S3 as the object store type

!!! warning "Destination Name Consistency"
 Ensure the destination names are identical, if SAP Build and Joule are in separate subaccount.

Step 2: Create Document Grounding Resource Group in Joule Studio


Now that the SAP AI Core infrastructure is ready, configure document grounding in Joule Studio.

2.1 Access Document Grounding in Control Tower

a. Open SAP Build Lobby
b. Navigate to Control Tower
c. Select Document Grounding

2.2 Add a Resource Group

a. Click Add to create a new resource group
b. In the Add Resource Group dialog:

 Step i: Name
- Enter a descriptive name (e.g., `Product Documentation`)
- Click Next

 Step ii: Select AI Core Destination
- Destination Type: `HTTP`
- Authentication Type: `OAuth2ClientCredentials`
- Select your AI Core destination: `ai-core`
- Click Next

 Step iii: Select Object Store Destination
- Destination Type: `HTTP`
- Authentication Type: `OAuth2ClientCredentials`
- Select your object store destination: `object-store-s3`
- Click Next

c. Click Add to create the resource group

3.3 Monitor Resource Group Status

After creation, the resource group will go through a grounding process:

 

StatusDescriptionAction Required
PendingGrounding process is runningWait for completion
CompletedReady to use Can be added to agents
ErrorSomething went wrongCheck logs and retry


!!! info "Grounding Process"
The grounding process creates the necessary connections and validates that the AI Core resource group and collections are accessible. This may take a few minutes.

3.4 Manage Resource Groups

Once created, you can perform the following actions on resource groups:

  • Edit: Modify the resource group configuration
  • Refresh: Re-run the grounding process (useful if you've added new documents)
  • Delete: Remove the resource group


!!! note "Refresh vs New Documents"
 The Refresh action refers to re-running the grounding process in Joule Studio, not refreshing the AI Core resources. To add new documents, add them via AI Core API (Step 2.4), then refresh the resource group in Joule Studio.

Step 3: Create a Joule Agent

Now create a Joule agent that will use document grounding capabilities.

3.1 Create the Agent

a. In your Joule Studio project, click Create → Joule Agent
b. Name: Enter a descriptive name (e.g., `Product Support Agent`)
c. Description: Provide a meaningful description:
 ```
This agent assists customers with product-related questions by searching through
product documentation, manuals, and technical guides to provide accurate,
contextual answers.
 ```
d. Click Create

3.2 Configure Agent Expertise

The Expertise field defines the agent's role and focus area. Use role prompting to make the agent more specialized.

Example:
```
You are a knowledgeable product support specialist with expertise in our enterprise
software products. You provide accurate, helpful answers based on official product
documentation.
```
Best Practices:

  • Be short and clear (2-3 sentences)
  • Define a specific role or persona
  • Avoid being too general
  • Focus on the agent's domain expertise
3.3 Configure Agent Instructions

The Instructions field defines what tasks the agent should perform. Leave empty for conversational agents, or provide specific instructions for structured, repetitive tasks.

Example for Document Search Agent:
```
When a user asks a question:
1. Use the document search tool to find relevant information from the documentation
2. Read the retrieved content carefully
3. Provide a clear, accurate answer based on the documentation
4. If information is not found in the documents, clearly state that
5. Include relevant context and examples from the documentation when helpful
```

!!! warning "Conversational vs Structured"
- Conversational agents: Leave Instructions empty to allow adaptive responses
- Structured workflows: Provide detailed, step-by-step instructions

3.4 Add Additional Context

Use the Additional Context field to provide guidance on specific situations. All additional context should begin with `##`.

Example:

```markdown
##Tool Usage Guidelines
When using the document search tool, prefer specific search queries over broad ones.
For technical questions, prioritize searching for exact error codes or feature names.

## Response Formatting
When providing installation instructions, always format steps as numbered lists.
When referencing specific product versions, always include the version number.

## Handling Missing Information
If the documentation does not contain the answer, use the Final Answer tool to inform
the user and suggest alternative resources such as contacting support directly.
```

Prompting Guide: https://pages.github.tools.sap/ICN-AI-Agents/baf-documentation/documentation/general/prompting/

3.5 Configure Model Settings

a. LLM Provider: Choose your LLM provider (e.g., OpenAI, Azure OpenAI)
b. Base Model: Select the model for writing responses (e.g., `gpt-4o`)
c. Advanced Model: Select the model for planning and tool selection (e.g., `gpt-4o`)
d. Backup LLM: (Optional) Enable a backup LLM for resilience

!!! info "Model Postfixes"
- `-minimal`: Nearly no reasoning
- `-low`: Basic reasoning
- `-auto`: Model decides reasoning depth

3.6 Configure Agent Execution Steps
a. Maximum Number of Thinking Steps: Set to `10-15` for document search agents
  • Higher values allow more complex searches and reasoning
  • Lower values are faster but may miss complex queries

b. Pre-processing Steps: Enable (recommended)

  • Includes direct instructions, task decomposition, and planning
  • Helps break down complex questions

c. Post-processing Steps: Enable (recommended)

  •  Includes refinement and answer bundling
  •  Provides cleaner, more coherent responses
3.7 Configure Agent Output
a. Default Output Format:      
  • Text: Plain text (simple responses)
  • Markdown: Formatted text (recommended for document grounding)
  • JSON: Structured data
  • Data Type: Structured data with scheme

b. Allow Joule to interpret the output: Enable for conversational agents

  • Joule may rephrase or summarize responses
  • Disable for technical accuracy requirements

!!! success "Recommended Settings for Document Grounding"
- Output Format: Markdown (allows formatted responses with lists, headings, code blocks)
- Interpret Output: Enabled (for more natural conversations)

Step 4: Add Document Grounding Tool to Agent

Now integrate the document grounding tool with your Joule agent.

4.1 Add the Documents Tool

a. In your Joule agent configuration, scroll to the Tools section
b. Click Add Tool → Documents

4.2 Configure the Documents Tool

In the Add Tool dialog:
a. Name: Enter a descriptive name for the tool

Example:

```
Product Documentation Search
```

b. Description: Provide a clear description of what the tool searches

Example:

```
Searches through product documentation, user manuals, installation guides, and
technical specifications to find relevant information about our enterprise products.
Use this tool when answering questions about product features, installation,
configuration, troubleshooting, or best practices.
```

!!! tip "Description Best Practices"
- Be specific about what content is available
- Mention key topic areas covered
- Include when the tool should be used
- Use clear, actionable language

c. Resource Group: Select the resource group you created in Step 3

Only resource groups with status Completed are available. Example: `Product Documentation`

d. Click Add

5.3 Verify Tool Addition

After adding the tool, you should see it listed in the Tools section of your agent configuration:

  • Tool Name: `Product Documentation Search`
  • Tool Type: `Documents`
  • Resource Group: `Product Documentation`

You can edit or remove the tool at any time by clicking the corresponding icons.

Debugging & Issues:

  • Use the Trace Feature

Use Joule Studio's trace feature to debug and optimize:

a. Open the agent chat interface
b. Enable Trace or Debug Mode
c. Ask a question
d. Review: 

Which tools were called 

What search queries were used 

What documents were retrieved 

How the agent reasoned through the response 

  • Issue: Agent Doesn't Use Document Search Tool

Symptoms: Agent provides answers without searching documents

Possible Causes & Solutions:

a. Vague Tool Description: Make tool description more specific and actionable
```
Bad: "Search documents"
Good: "Searches product documentation including installation guides, user manuals,
and API references. Use this tool when answering product-related questions."
 ```

b. Missing Instructions: Add explicit instructions to use the tool
```
"When answering questions, use the 'Product Documentation Search' tool to find
relevant information before responding."
 ```

c. Too Many Tools: If agent has many tools, prioritize document search in instructions

For more troubleshooting refer this link: https://pages.github.tools.sap/ICN-AI-Agents/baf-documentation/documentation/ui-walkthrough/troubleshoot/

Reference Document: https://help.sap.com/docs/Joule_Studio/45f9d2b8914b4f0ba731570ff9a85313/4444cd1ce4cd471bbe127ea2e4735b40.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: SAP Signavio + SAP Cloud ALM: Connecting Process Design to Implementation
entry.content:
We started this integrated toolchain series with an intro blog on why SAP transformation programs that have the right tools but haven't connected them are leaving value on the table, and what the Digital Thread looks like when they do.  In Blog 1, we connected LeanIX to Cloud ALM giving Enterprise Architects an automatically maintained view of the SAP landscape. In Blog 2, we connected LeanIX to Signavio aligning the application portfolio with the business process model. This Blog 3 takes the next step: connecting SAP Signavio directly to SAP Cloud ALM, so that the to-be processes your business defines don't stay in a process tool so, they become the implementation blueprint your IT team works from.

 

The Gap Between Process Design and Implementation

Most S/4HANA transformation programs have a business stream and an IT stream. The business stream defines and documents to-be processes what the organisation wants to do differently after the transformation. The IT stream implements those processes in the system with configuration, testing, deployment.

These two streams (IT & Business) are rarely as connected as they should be. Process designs get documented in Signavio, then manually exported, shared, interpreted, and re-entered by implementation teams in Cloud ALM. By the time the implementation consultant is working from a process model, it may already be two versions out of date. Requirements get lost in translation. Fit-to-standard workshops produce decisions that don't make it into the implementation backlog

The business defined the process. IT implemented something slightly different.

Nobody noticed until go-live.

The Signavio - Cloud ALM integration eliminates this translation layer. To-be process models defined and approved in Signavio sync directly into Cloud ALM, where implementation teams can work from them immediately and navigate back to the source when anything needs clarification.

What the Integration Does

The standard integration between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM was released to general availability in Q4 2024. It connects the two platforms via SAP-managed connectivity  no custom development required  and delivers a set of capabilities that directly support the implementation phase of an S/4HANA transformation. 

For detailed information regarding the integration, refer to the following resources:

  • SAP Community : OUT NOW: Standard integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud ALM → [link] by @danielafabbrin40
  • SAP Help : Getting Started Guide → [link]
  • Session slide deck : L1 Deck → [link]
  • SAP Road Map Explorer →  [link]
  • Session recording : Accelerate Your Business Transformation with SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM Integration → [link] Check it out below presented by Simon Blattmann at SAP.

Syncing To-Be Process Models

The core capability: process owners can sync their latest to-be BPMN diagrams from the SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub directly to SAP Cloud ALM. The sync is triggered from the Signavio Hub UI , the process owner opens a diagram in Preview mode and triggers the sync to Cloud ALM with a single action. No export, no manual upload, no email.

Once synced, the to-be process model appears in Cloud ALM as a Solution Process with a corresponding Solution Process Flow Diagram  ready for the implementation team to work from. The mapping is 1:1: one BPMN diagram in Signavio creates one Solution Process and one Solution Process Flow Diagram in Cloud ALM

Bidirectional Navigation

Once the sync is complete, both teams can navigate between the two tools directly. Process owners in Signavio can jump to the corresponding Solution Process Diagram in Cloud ALM. Implementation consultants in Cloud ALM can navigate back to the source process model in Signavio ,so when a question arises about what a process step means, the answer is one click away, not one email thread away.

Implementation Control

When a to-be process model is synced into Cloud ALM, the Solution Process Flow Diagram is blocked for editing in Cloud ALM. This is a deliberate design decision: Signavio remains the system of record for process design. Changes to the process happen in Signavio, not in Cloud ALM. When the process model is updated and re-synced, Cloud ALM reflects the change. This keeps the two platforms in sync and prevents implementation teams from making undocumented process changes that diverge from the agreed design.

 

What the integration delivers

• Sync latest to-be BPMN process models from Signavio Process Collaboration Hub to Cloud ALM

• 1:1 mapping: one Signavio diagram → one Cloud ALM Solution Process + one Solution Process Flow Diagram

• Navigate from Signavio to the corresponding Cloud ALM Solution Process Diagram and back

• Cloud ALM Solution Process Flow Diagram blocked for editing. Signavio remains the system of record

• SAP-managed connectivity via SAP for Me , no custom development required.

• Roadmap: harmonized process hierarchy, extended lifecycle control, sync status visibility at diagram level.

The Three-Role User Flow

The integration is designed around three roles, each with a distinct responsibility in the process-to-implementation handover:

IT System Owner

IT System Owner sets up the connectivity between the Signavio workspace and the Cloud ALM tenant via SAP for Me. they trigger provisioning of the automated connectivity add-on, confirms the connection and informs the process owner when the system is ready. This is a one-time setup per workspace.

Process Owner

Process Owner works in Signavio. When a to-be process model is ready ,reviewed, approved, and published. they open it in the Process Collaboration Hub in Preview mode and triggers the sync to Cloud ALM. They can see the sync status and navigate directly to Cloud ALM to confirm the model has landed correctly.

Implementation Consultant

Implementation Consultant works in Cloud ALM. She receives the synced process model as a Solution Process and uses it as the basis for fit-to-standard workshops, requirement capture, and solution design. If they have a question about a process step, they navigate back to Signavio directly from Cloud ALM. If the process changes, the next sync from Signavio updates Cloud ALM automatically.

 

The Handover That Actually Works

Consider a transformation program where the business has spent six weeks in fit-to-standard workshops defining 40 to-be processes in Signavio. In a traditional setup, those processes now need to be exported, shared with the implementation team, and manually re-entered or referenced in Cloud ALM a process that takes days and introduces version risk.

With the integration active, the process owner triggers a sync from Signavio. All 40 diagrams appear in Cloud ALM within minutes, correctly structured and ready to work from. The implementation consultant opens a process in Cloud ALM, has a question about a specific step, and navigates directly to the Signavio model to review the context. No email. No waiting. No version mismatch.

When the business updates three processes after a workshop, the process owner re-syncs. Cloud ALM reflects the changes. The implementation team is always working from the current approved design.

Why It Matters for S/4HANA Transformations

Process-Driven Implementation

The integration enforces a principle that most transformation methodologies espouse but few programs actually achieve: the business defines the process, IT implements it. By making Signavio the system of record and blocking Cloud ALM from independently editing synced process diagrams, the integration structurally prevents the implementation from drifting away from the agreed design.

Clean Core Alignment

The integration aligns directly with SAP's Clean Core strategy. To-be processes defined in Signavio should reflect SAP standard processes. The integration supports this by making it easier for implementation teams to see exactly what the business has agreed to, before any configuration or extension decisions are made. Deviations from standard become visible as deliberate decisions, not accidental drift.

Fit-to-Standard Workshop Efficiency

Fit-to-standard workshops are where transformation programs make their most consequential decisions. The integration means the output of those workshops approved to-be process models in Signavio flows directly into Cloud ALM without manual re-entry. Workshop outcomes become implementation inputs on the same day.

Reduced Rework Risk

Implementation rework caused by misaligned process documentation is one of the most common and expensive failure modes in SAP transformation programs. When the implementation team is working from a process model that is one version behind what the business agreed to, the consequences show up at testing or go-live. The continuous sync between Signavio and Cloud ALM removes this risk by ensuring both teams are always working from the same source.

Setup and Connectivity

The connection is established via SAP for Me. The connectivity add-on for SAP Signavio solutions is automatically subscribed when both the Signavio workspace and the Cloud ALM tenant exist under the same business partner ID. Setup involves triggering provisioning in SAP for Me, selecting the SAP Cloud Identity service and the Signavio system, and confirming the connection to the Cloud ALM tenant.

One important constraint: the connectivity is one-to-one. One Signavio workspace connects to one Cloud ALM tenant. Multiple Signavio workspaces connecting to the same Cloud ALM tenant, or connecting workspaces and tenants under different business partner IDs, is not currently supported.

 

Setup & Learning Resources

• SAP Help Portal: Integrating SAP Signavio Process Manager with SAP Cloud ALM: Link 

• SAP Enable Now : Click-through demo (Signavio–Cloud ALM integration): Link 

• SAP Community : Initial Setup: Preparing for SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio Integration:Link 

• SAP Community : Synchronization in Action: Executing the Integration Between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio: Link 

• SAP Road Map Explorer : Signavio + Cloud ALM roadmap:  [link]

Have you activated the Signavio-Cloud ALM integration in your transformation program? What changed in the handover between your business and IT teams? Share your experience in the comments ,I'd love to hear from you.

Next: Blog 4 - LeanIX + SAP Signavio + SAP Cloud ALM: The Combined View

Blogs 1, 2, and 3 each activated one integration. Blog 4 steps back and looks at all three together a review of what we have built so far and what the Digital Thread looks like when LeanIX, SAP Signavio, and SAP Cloud ALM are all connected and working as one.

Ramy Salem

ANZ Toolchain Ambassador at SAP

PhD Candidate – AI Agents for Enterprise Architecture

Certified in SAP | WalkMe | LeanIX | Signavio   ·   TOGAF Certified

Read the Toolchain Blogs that inspired this series:

  • Why the SAP Integrated Toolchain Is the Future of Digital Transformation
  • Integrated Toolchain: LeanIX + SAP Cloud ALM: Auto-Discovering Your SAP Landscape 
  • LeanIX + SAP Signavio: Connecting Your Application Portfolio to Your Business Processes 
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with WalkMe in SAP Landscapes
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with SAP Signavio in SAP Landscapes
  • Bridging Strategy to Execution: How LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe Create the Ultimate EA Toolchain

entry.title: Re: How correct data on completed I-9 Form and E-Verify i...
entry.content: Hi Heather - we had a case with a TNC because a date of birth was incorrect and followed the SAP steps which closed the case. Your instructions were super helpful and I was able to trigger the I-9 flow again to get the data corrected. But it did not re-trigger the E-verify process. Do you know how to send the corrected I-9 through to E-verify to open a new case since the TNC does not provide an employment eligibility status.
